PAGE 1 | CONFIDENTIAL | MASTER REVOLVING CREDIT AND OVERDRAFT FACILITY 
 AGREEMENT 
 e-Stamp Certificate No: IN-GJ55443322110099Y 
 Date of Issuance: 12-Nov-2025 10:14 AM 
 Stamp Duty Amount: INR 1,50,000.00 
 Purchaser: OMEGA-FORGE DYNAMICS PRIVATE LIMITED 
 Vendor: AUTHORIZED STAMP VENDOR, NAVRANPURA, AHMEDABAD 
 S.NO. 112-Q. A H M E D A B A D. 
 MASTER REVOLVING CREDIT AND SECURED OVERDRAFT FACILITY 
 AGREEMENT 
 THIS MASTER REVOLVING CREDIT AND SECURED OVERDRAFT FACILITY 
 AGREEMENT (hereinafter referred to as the "Agreement" or "Deed of Credit") is made, 
 executed and entered into on this 14th day of November, 2025 ("Execution Date") at 
 Ahmedabad, Gujarat, India, with absolute and retroactive effect from 01/10/2025 (the 
 "Effective Date"). 
 BY AND AMONGST 
 VAKRATUNDA CAPITAL SERVICES LIMITED, a Non-Banking Financial Company 
 (NBFC) incorporated under the Companies Act, 1956, and registered with the Reserve 
 Bank of India (RBI Registration No. B-14.00987), bearing Corporate Identification 
 Number (CIN) L65910GJ1992PLC054321, having its Registered and Corporate Office 
 at 9th Floor, Shikhar Tower, SG Highway, Bodakdev, Ahmedabad, Gujarat 380054, 
 India (Email ID: credit.sanctions@vakratundacapital.in; Ph: +91-79-26554433), bearing 
 GST Number 24AAACV1111E1Z8 and PAN AAACV1111E (hereinafter referred to as the 
 "Lender" or "Financial Institution", which expression shall, unless it be repugnant to 
 the context, meaning or strict interpretation thereof, be deemed to mean and include 
 its successors, assignees, administrators, and permitted transferees) of the FIRST 
 PART; 
 AND 
 OMEGA-FORGE DYNAMICS PRIVATE LIMITED, a company incorporated under the 
 Companies Act, 2013, bearing Corporate Identification Number (CIN) 
 U28110GJ2018PTC098765, having its Registered Office at Plot No. 88-A, Phase-IV, 
 GIDC Estate, Vatva, Ahmedabad, Gujarat 382445, India (Email ID: 
 finance@omegaforge.co.in; Ph: 079-44556677), bearing GST Number 
 24BBZPO9988Q1Z4 and PAN BBZPO9988Q (hereinafter referred to as the "Borrower", 
 which expression shall, unless it be repugnant to the context or meaning thereof, be 
 deemed to mean and include its directors, legal heirs, successors, and permitted 
 assigns) of the SECOND PART; 
 AND 
 MRS. SAVITRI CHATTOPADHYAY, W/o Dr. Alok Chattopadhyay, aged about 61 years, 
 residing at Bungalow No. 4, Sterling City, Bopal, Ahmedabad 380058, holding 
 Passport Number M7766554, Aadhaar Number [Aadhaar Redacted], Voter ID 
 GJ/01/045/112233, and Mobile Number +91-9898989898 (hereinafter referred to as the 
 "Guarantor") of the THIRD PART; 
 AND 
 M/S SENTINEL ASSET RECONSTRUCTION & TRUSTEESHIP COMPANY, a statutory 
 body corporate, having its regional office at 3rd Floor, Vasant Vihar, New Delhi 110057
(Email: recovery@sentineltrust.org), bearing Registration Number Reg-TR-2021-008 
 (hereinafter referred to as the "Security Trustee") of the FOURTH PART. 
 (The Lender, Borrower, Guarantor, and Security Trustee are hereinafter collectively 
 referred to as the "Parties" and individually as a "Party"). 
 R E C I T A L S 
 WHEREAS, the Borrower is engaged inter alia in the highly capital-intensive business 
 of manufacturing precision drop-forged automobile components, industrial flanges, 
 and titanium alloy casts under Factory License No. FL-GJ-2018-998 issued by the 
 Directorate of Industrial Safety and Health, Government of Gujarat. 
 WHEREAS, the Borrower, via Board Resolution dated 2025-09-15, resolved to seek 
 working capital enhancement and has formally approached the Lender vide Loan 
 Application Number App/VCSL/25/0892 for a revolving credit and secured overdraft 
 facility to fulfill its working capital requirements, supplier payments (Vendor IDs: 
 V-O-112, V-O-445), and capital expenditure outlays. 
 WHEREAS, pursuant to the Credit Sanction Letter No. VCSL/SANC/2025/11/44 dated 
 October 25, 2025, the Lender has agreed bona fide to grant to the Borrower a 
 Multi-Currency Revolving Credit Facility up to an aggregate maximum limit of ₹ 
 45,00,00,000 (Rupees Forty-Five Crores Only) and a sub-limit of AED 5,000,000 (UAE 
 Dirhams Five Million), subject strictly to the stringent terms, conditions, hidden 
 charges, and penal obligations stipulated herein below. 
 NOW, THEREFORE, IN CONSIDERATION OF THE PREMISES, MUTUAL COVENANTS, 
 OBLIGATIONS AND PROMISES CONTAINED HEREIN, IT IS HEREBY AGREED BY AND 
 BETWEEN THE PARTIES AS FOLLOWS: 
 1. DEFINITIONS AND PRINCIPLES OF INTERPRETATION 
 1.1. Definitions 
 In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise dictates, the following terms shall 
 have the specific meanings assigned to them: 
 ● "Availability Period" shall mean the period commencing from 01.XI.2025 and 
 ending exactly on 31/10/2028, beyond which the facility shall automatically 
 expire unless silently renewed pursuant to Clause 4.1. 
 ● "Drawing Power" (DP) shall mean the permissible limit of withdrawal calculated 
 monthly as 60% of eligible book debts (not older than 90 days) and 50% of paid 
 inventory (Invoice Numbers INV-OF-001 to INV-OF-899), strictly certified by the 
 Lender's impaneled valuer. 
 ● "Event of Default" shall have the exhaustive meaning ascribed to it in Clause 8 
 hereof, encompassing both financial and non-financial breaches. 
 ● "Force Majeure" shall mean any event outside the reasonable control of the 
 Parties, including acts of God, war, pandemic, but ipso facto and ab initio 
 expressly excluding changes in economic conditions, raw material price 
 fluctuations, currency embargoes, or the insolvency/bankruptcy of the 
 Borrower. 
 ● "PLR" shall mean the Prime Lending Rate unilaterally declared by the Lender 
 from time to time without obligation of public notification. 
 1.2. Interpretation 
 Words importing the singular shall include the plural and mutatis mutandis . 
 References to any statute shall include all amendments, notifications, and
re-enactments thereof. The rule of contra proferentem shall not apply against the 
 Lender in the interpretation of this Deed. 
 2. THE CREDIT FACILITY AND DRAWDOWN CONDITIONS 
 2.1. Quantum of Facility 
 Subject strictly to the terms herein, the Lender agrees to provide a Secured Overdraft 
 (OD) and Revolving Credit Facility (the "Facility") comprising: 
 (a) Tranche A (Rupee OD Limit): Up to ₹ 35,00,00,000 (Rupees Thirty-Five Crores). 
 (b) Tranche B (Foreign Currency Demand Loan): Up to USD 1,200,000 (US Dollars One 
 Million Two Hundred Thousand) for import L/C discounting under Tender No. 
 TND-IMP-2025-99. 
 2.2. Disbursement and Routing 
 The Borrower shall ensure that all drawdowns are credited strictly to the 
 Lender-controlled Escrow Account (Account No. 88990011223344, Vakratunda Capital, 
 SG Highway Branch, IFSC: VCSL0000111, SWIFT: VCSLINBBAXXX). Any diversion of 
 funds to other accounts (e.g., the Borrower's pre-existing account at Bank of Baroda) 
 shall constitute an immediate Event of Default. 
 2.3. Purpose Restriction 
 The Borrower undertakes to utilize the Facility solely for the purchase of raw 
 materials from approved suppliers (e.g., Tata Steel Ltd., Vendor ID V-776) and strictly 
 prohibits the use of funds for capital market speculation, real estate acquisition, or 
 repayment of unsecured loans extended by the Directors. 
 P A G E 3 -- S C A N R E A D -- E R R O R 0 x 0 F F T h e 
 B o r r o w e r s h a l l s u b m i t t h e r e p o r t w i t h i n 
 7 d a y s . 
 3. INTEREST, HIDDEN CHARGES, AND OVERDUE PAYMENT PENALTIES 
 3.1. Standard Interest Rates 
 (a) Tranche A shall bear interest at a floating rate of PLR + 4.50% spread, currently 
 equating to 14.75% per annum, calculated on a daily debit balance basis and applied 
 monthly on the 28th day of every calendar month. 
 (b) Tranche B shall bear interest at 3-month SOFR + 600 bps, payable quarterly. 
 (c) Unilateral Reset: The Lender reserves the absolute, unfettered right to 
 retrospectively reset the spread by up to 2.00% every six months without prior notice 
 to the Borrower, notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained herein. 
 3.2. Hidden Charges and Deductions (The "Fee Trap") 
 The Borrower hereby irrevocably agrees to pay, and authorizes the Lender to 
 auto-debit from the Escrow Account, the following non-refundable charges: 
 (a) Processing & Syndication Fee: 2.5% of the total sanctioned limit (₹ 1,12,50,000) 
 payable upfront (Invoice No. VCSL-FEE-25/001). 
 (b) Annual Maintenance Charge (AMC): A hidden facility maintenance charge of ₹ 
 1,50,000 per month, arbitrarily categorized as 'administrative overhead', billed 
 continuously regardless of facility utilization. 
 (c) Document Retrieval Fee: ₹ 25,000 per instance whenever the Borrower requests 
 copies of statements, NOCs, or collateral title deeds. 
 (d) Statutory Compliance Audit Fee: An annual charge of USD 5,000 equivalent, 
 payable every March 15th, purportedly for verifying the Borrower's environmental 
 compliance (e.g., MPCB Certificate No. MPCB/2024/991).
(e) Inactive Account Fee: If the average utilization drops below 30% of the Sanctioned 
 Limit for a continuous period of 45 days, a "Capital Blockage Penalty" of 1.5% per 
 month on the unutilized amount shall be levied ex parte . 
 3.3. Punitive Overdue Payment Charges (Draconian Default Penalty) 
 It is expressly agreed that strict adherence to the payment schedule is the essence of 
 this Agreement. In the event the Borrower fails to service the interest, principal, or 
 any fee by the due date (even by a single day): 
 (a) Compounding Default Interest: The overdue amount shall attract a penal interest 
 rate of 4% PER MONTH (approx. 48% per annum), compounded WEEKLY until fully 
 regularized. 
 (b) Flat Instance Penalty: A flat punitive charge of ₹ 50,000 (Rupees Fifty Thousand) 
 shall be levied automatically for every single instance of payment failure, dishonored 
 cheque (e.g., Cheque No. 445566 drawn on HDFC Bank), or NACH mandate bounce. 
 (c) Recovery Agent Deployment Fee: Upon an overdue status exceeding 15 days, the 
 Lender may deploy field recovery agents. The Borrower shall reimburse a "Recovery 
 Deployment Fee" of ₹ 15,000 per day per agent, plus travel expenses. 
 4. OBLIGATIONS, UNDERTAKINGS, AND COVENANTS 
 4.1. Positive Obligations 
 The Borrower shall and hereby strictly undertakes to: 
 (a) Maintain a Current Ratio of not less than 1.33:1 and a Total Outside Liabilities to 
 Tangible Net Worth (TOL/TNW) ratio not exceeding 2.5:1. 
 (b) Ensure that all cash flows generated from operations (including Contract No. 
 CON-BHEL-2025/11) are routed exclusively through the Lender's Escrow Account. 
 (c) Insure all hypothecated assets (including Plant & Machinery at Survey No. 45/1) at 
 its own cost for 130% of their market value against all risks, assigning the policy 
 (Policy No. IFFCO-TOK-998877) in favor of the Security Trustee. 
 (d) Submit audited financials, GST Returns (GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B), and CA-certified 
 stock statements by the 10th of every succeeding month. 
 (e) Immediately notify the Lender of any material litigation, specifically updating the 
 status of Case No. NCLT/AHM/CP/112/2024 pending before the National Company Law 
 Tribunal. 
 4.2. Negative Covenants 
 Without the prior written, explicitly sealed consent of the Lender, the Borrower is 
 expressly prohibited from and shall not: 
 (a) Undertake any expansion, merger, amalgamation, or new capital expenditure 
 exceeding ₹ 25,00,000. 
 (b) Declare, make, or pay any dividend or distribute profits to its shareholders. 
 (c) Change its shareholding pattern, Board of Directors, or key managerial personnel. 
 (d) Avail any further financial assistance, credit, or loan from any bank, NBFC, or 
 individual, whether secured or unsecured. 
 (e) Enter into any settlement with the labor union (Union Reg No. LBR-GJ-554). 
 5. HIDDEN LEGAL TRAPS & HOSTILE STRUCTURING 
 5.1. Silent & Automatic Renewal (Negative Option Renewal) 
 Notwithstanding anything contained in Clause 1.1, upon the expiration of the 
 Availability Period (31-Oct-2028), this Agreement shall ipso facto and automatically 
 renew for a successive period of 36 months unless the Borrower issues a written 
 "Notice of Non-Renewal" via Registered Post with Acknowledgment Due (RPAD)
exactly between the 180th day and 175th day prior to the expiry. Any notice issued 
 outside this narrow 5-day window shall be deemed invalid. Upon automatic renewal, 
 the Processing Fee (Clause 3.2.a) shall be levied again at a hiked rate of 3.5%. 
 5.2. Cross Default and Acceleration 
 The Borrower explicitly agrees that a default by the Borrower, its subsidiaries, sister 
 concerns, or the Guarantor under any other agreement with any third-party creditor 
 (regardless of the amount, e.g., a delayed telecom bill of ₹ 5,000) shall constitute a 
 Cross Default under this Agreement, entitling the Lender to immediately accelerate 
 the loan and demand full repayment within 48 hours. 
 5.3. Absolute Assignment of Receivables and IP 
 As additional security, the Borrower hereby executes an ab initio absolute assignment 
 of all its present and future trade receivables. Furthermore, in the event of default, the 
 Borrower automatically assigns all rights, title, and interest in its proprietary patents 
 (Patent No. IN-2023-887766 for 'Thermal Forging Process') to the Lender without 
 requirement of further deed or court order. 
 5.4. Unilateral Right to Debit and Set-Off 
 The Lender shall have a paramount, continuous, and overarching right of set-off and 
 lien, independent of any other encumbrance, over all deposits, fixed deposits (FD 
 Receipt No. FDR-99887766), mutual fund units, and any other assets of the Borrower 
 and Guarantor held with any financial institution in India, to appropriate towards the 
 outstanding dues without prior notice. 
 P A G E 6 -- F O R M A T B R E A K I n i t i a l s : ______ I 
 n i t i a l s : ______ 
 6. SECURITY AND HYPOTHECATION 
 The Facility shall be secured by: 
 6.1. Primary Security: A first pari passu charge by way of hypothecation over all entire 
 current assets of the Borrower, including raw materials, work-in-progress, finished 
 goods, consumable stores, and spares lying at the Vatva plant (Property ID: 
 VAT-99-GIDC). 
 6.2. Collateral Security: A registered equitable mortgage over the commercial real 
 estate owned by the Guarantor located at Plot 44, SG Highway, Ahmedabad (Survey 
 No. 112/A, Khata No. 889). 
 6.3. Personal Guarantee: The absolute, irrevocable, and unconditional personal 
 guarantee of Mrs. Savitri Chattopadhyay. The Guarantor agrees that her liability is 
 co-extensive with the Borrower and the Lender may proceed directly against her 
 without first exhausting remedies against the Borrower. 
 7. FORCE MAJEURE EXCEPTIONS (Pro-Lender) 
 The obligations stipulated under Clause 3 (Interest & Payments) and Clause 4 
 (Obligations) shall continue mutatis mutandis notwithstanding any Force Majeure 
 event. The Borrower's obligation to repay borrowed money and accrue interest is 
 absolute and shall not be suspended by any pandemic, lockdown, government order, 
 or "Act of God". 
 8. EVENTS OF DEFAULT 
 The occurrence of any of the following shall constitute an Event of Default: 
 8.1. Failure to pay any principal, interest, overdue charge, hidden fee, or penalty on 
 the exact due date.
8.2. Any representation, warranty, or financial statement (including the Audit Report 
 dated 12.V.2025) is found to be incorrect, misleading, or deceptive prima facie . 
 8.3. The Borrower’s net worth erodes by more than 15% in any single financial 
 quarter. 
 8.4. A distress, execution, attachment, or other legal process is levied against any 
 asset of the Borrower (e.g., Seizure of Vehicle Number GJ-01-XX-9999 by RTO 
 authorities). 
 8.5. The Guarantor is declared insolvent, attempts to alienate the mortgaged property, 
 or passes away. 
 9. GOVERNING LAW, ARBITRATION AND JURISDICTION 
 9.1. Governing Law 
 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed strictly in accordance with the 
 laws of the Republic of India. 
 9.2. Arbitration (Mandatory and Asymmetric) 
 Notwithstanding anything contained in the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, any 
 dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, 
 including any question regarding its existence, validity, or termination, shall be 
 referred to and finally resolved by arbitration. 
 Trap: The arbitral tribunal shall consist of a Sole Arbitrator to be unilaterally 
 appointed by the Lender. The seat, venue, and place of arbitration shall exclusively be 
 GIFT City, Gandhinagar, Gujarat. All proceedings shall be in English. The Borrower 
 explicitly waives its right to seek interim relief from any civil court. 
 9.3. Exclusive Jurisdiction 
 Subject to the arbitration clause above, the courts at Ahmedabad alone shall have 
 exclusive jurisdiction to try and entertain any disputes. The Borrower expressly 
 waives the defense of forum non conveniens . 
 10. AMENDMENTS AND REVISIONS 
 10.1. Amendment No.1 (Pre-Execution Insertion) 
 It is mutually agreed that Clause 3.2(a) stands amended ab initio . The Processing Fee 
 is hereby increased from 2.5% to 3.0% (₹ 1,35,00,000) effective immediately. 
 10.2. Deleted Clause 
 Clause 4.1(f) regarding "Borrower's right to request interest rate reduction" is hereby 
 deleted in its entirety and superseded by Clause 3.1(c). 
 10.3. Inserted Clause 8.6 
 "If the Borrower attempts to refinance this Facility through any other institution, a 
 'Refinancing Sabotage Penalty' of 8% of the sanctioned limit shall become 
 immediately due and payable."

 SIGNATURE BLOCKS, SEALS AND EXECUTION 
 IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties hereto have, acting through their duly authorized 
 representatives, executed and delivered this Master Revolving Credit and Secured 
 Overdraft Facility Agreement on the Execution Date and at the place first hereinabove 
 mentioned. 
 For and on behalf of THE LENDER: 
 VAKRATUNDA CAPITAL SERVICES LIMITED 
 Signature: ___________________________ 
 Name: Mr. Harshad Mehta 
 Designation: Chief Risk Officer 
 Employee ID: VCSL-EMP-0012 
 Date: 14/11/2025 
 Company Seal: [ CORPORATE SEAL AFFIXED ] 
 For and on behalf of THE BORROWER: 
 OMEGA-FORGE DYNAMICS PRIVATE LIMITED 
 Signature: ___________________________
Name: Mr. Jignesh Patel 
 Designation: Managing Director 
 DIN: 00998877

 SENTINEL ASSET RECONSTRUCTION & TRUSTEESHIP CO.

 Location: Civil Court Premises, Ahmedabad. 
 [ ROUND NOTARY STAMP AFFIXED ] 
 [ RED WAFER SEAL AFFIXED ] 
 --- END OF DEED OF CREDIT ---

 ● "PLR" shall mean the Prime Lending Rate unilaterally declared by the Lender 
 from time to time without obligation of public notification. 
 1.2. Interpretation 
 Words importing the singular shall include the plural and mutatis mutandis . 
 References to any statute shall include all amendments, notifications, and

documents.page_content:
re-enactments thereof. The rule of contra proferentem shall not apply against the 
 Lender in the interpretation of this Deed. 
 2. THE CREDIT FACILITY AND DRAWDOWN CONDITIONS 
 2.1. Quantum of Facility 
 Subject strictly to the terms herein, the Lender agrees to provide a Secured Overdraft 
 (OD) and Revolving Credit Facility (the "Facility") comprising: 
 (a) Tranche A (Rupee OD Limit): Up to ₹ 35,00,00,000 (Rupees Thirty-Five Crores). 
 (b) Tranche B (Foreign Currency Demand Loan): Up to USD 1,200,000 (US Dollars One 
 Million Two Hundred Thousand) for import L/C discounting under Tender No. 
 TND-IMP-2025-99. 
 2.2. Disbursement and Routing 
 The Borrower shall ensure that all drawdowns are credited strictly to the 
 Lender-controlled Escrow Account (Account No. 88990011223344, Vakratunda Capital, 
 SG Highway Branch, IFSC: VCSL0000111, SWIFT: VCSLINBBAXXX). Any diversion of 
 funds to other accounts (e.g., the Borrower's pre-existing account at Bank of Baroda) 
 shall constitute an immediate Event of Default. 
 2.3. Purpose Restriction 
 The Borrower undertakes to utilize the Facility solely for the purchase of raw 
 materials from approved suppliers (e.g., Tata Steel Ltd., Vendor ID V-776) and strictly 
 prohibits the use of funds for capital market speculation, real estate acquisition, or 
 repayment of unsecured loans extended by the Directors. 
 P A G E 3 -- S C A N R E A D -- E R R O R 0 x 0 F F T h e 
 B o r r o w e r s h a l l s u b m i t t h e r e p o r t w i t h i n 
 7 d a y s . 
 3. INTEREST, HIDDEN CHARGES, AND OVERDUE PAYMENT PENALTIES 
 3.1. Standard Interest Rates 
 (a) Tranche A shall bear interest at a floating rate of PLR + 4.50% spread, currently 
 equating to 14.75% per annum, calculated on a daily debit balance basis and applied 
 monthly on the 28th day of every calendar month. 
 (b) Tranche B shall bear interest at 3-month SOFR + 600 bps, payable quarterly. 
 (c) Unilateral Reset: The Lender reserves the absolute, unfettered right to 
 retrospectively reset the spread by up to 2.00% every six months without prior notice 
 to the Borrower, notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained herein. 
 3.2. Hidden Charges and Deductions (The "Fee Trap") 
 The Borrower hereby irrevocably agrees to pay, and authorizes the Lender to 
 auto-debit from the Escrow Account, the following non-refundable charges: 
 (a) Processing & Syndication Fee: 2.5% of the total sanctioned limit (₹ 1,12,50,000) 
 payable upfront (Invoice No. VCSL-FEE-25/001). 
 (b) Annual Maintenance Charge (AMC): A hidden facility maintenance charge of ₹ 
 1,50,000 per month, arbitrarily categorized as 'administrative overhead', billed 
 continuously regardless of facility utilization. 
 (c) Document Retrieval Fee: ₹ 25,000 per instance whenever the Borrower requests 
 copies of statements, NOCs, or collateral title deeds. 
 (d) Statutory Compliance Audit Fee: An annual charge of USD 5,000 equivalent, 
 payable every March 15th, purportedly for verifying the Borrower's environmental 
 compliance (e.g., MPCB Certificate No. MPCB/2024/991).

metadata.creator: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/149.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

documents.page_content:
(e) Inactive Account Fee: If the average utilization drops below 30% of the Sanctioned 
 Limit for a continuous period of 45 days, a "Capital Blockage Penalty" of 1.5% per 
 month on the unutilized amount shall be levied ex parte . 
 3.3. Punitive Overdue Payment Charges (Draconian Default Penalty) 
 It is expressly agreed that strict adherence to the payment schedule is the essence of 
 this Agreement. In the event the Borrower fails to service the interest, principal, or 
 any fee by the due date (even by a single day): 
 (a) Compounding Default Interest: The overdue amount shall attract a penal interest 
 rate of 4% PER MONTH (approx. 48% per annum), compounded WEEKLY until fully 
 regularized. 
 (b) Flat Instance Penalty: A flat punitive charge of ₹ 50,000 (Rupees Fifty Thousand) 
 shall be levied automatically for every single instance of payment failure, dishonored 
 cheque (e.g., Cheque No. 445566 drawn on HDFC Bank), or NACH mandate bounce. 
 (c) Recovery Agent Deployment Fee: Upon an overdue status exceeding 15 days, the 
 Lender may deploy field recovery agents. The Borrower shall reimburse a "Recovery 
 Deployment Fee" of ₹ 15,000 per day per agent, plus travel expenses. 
 4. OBLIGATIONS, UNDERTAKINGS, AND COVENANTS 
 4.1. Positive Obligations 
 The Borrower shall and hereby strictly undertakes to: 
 (a) Maintain a Current Ratio of not less than 1.33:1 and a Total Outside Liabilities to 
 Tangible Net Worth (TOL/TNW) ratio not exceeding 2.5:1. 
 (b) Ensure that all cash flows generated from operations (including Contract No. 
 CON-BHEL-2025/11) are routed exclusively through the Lender's Escrow Account. 
 (c) Insure all hypothecated assets (including Plant & Machinery at Survey No. 45/1) at 
 its own cost for 130% of their market value against all risks, assigning the policy 
 (Policy No. IFFCO-TOK-998877) in favor of the Security Trustee. 
 (d) Submit audited financials, GST Returns (GSTR-1 and GSTR-3B), and CA-certified 
 stock statements by the 10th of every succeeding month. 
 (e) Immediately notify the Lender of any material litigation, specifically updating the 
 status of Case No. NCLT/AHM/CP/112/2024 pending before the National Company Law 
 Tribunal. 
 4.2. Negative Covenants 
 Without the prior written, explicitly sealed consent of the Lender, the Borrower is 
 expressly prohibited from and shall not: 
 (a) Undertake any expansion, merger, amalgamation, or new capital expenditure 
 exceeding ₹ 25,00,000. 
 (b) Declare, make, or pay any dividend or distribute profits to its shareholders. 
 (c) Change its shareholding pattern, Board of Directors, or key managerial personnel. 
 (d) Avail any further financial assistance, credit, or loan from any bank, NBFC, or 
 individual, whether secured or unsecured. 
 (e) Enter into any settlement with the labor union (Union Reg No. LBR-GJ-554). 
 5. HIDDEN LEGAL TRAPS & HOSTILE STRUCTURING 
 5.1. Silent & Automatic Renewal (Negative Option Renewal) 
 Notwithstanding anything contained in Clause 1.1, upon the expiration of the 
 Availability Period (31-Oct-2028), this Agreement shall ipso facto and automatically 
 renew for a successive period of 36 months unless the Borrower issues a written 
 "Notice of Non-Renewal" via Registered Post with Acknowledgment Due (RPAD)

metadata.creator: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/149.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

documents.page_content:
exactly between the 180th day and 175th day prior to the expiry. Any notice issued 
 outside this narrow 5-day window shall be deemed invalid. Upon automatic renewal, 
 the Processing Fee (Clause 3.2.a) shall be levied again at a hiked rate of 3.5%. 
 5.2. Cross Default and Acceleration 
 The Borrower explicitly agrees that a default by the Borrower, its subsidiaries, sister 
 concerns, or the Guarantor under any other agreement with any third-party creditor 
 (regardless of the amount, e.g., a delayed telecom bill of ₹ 5,000) shall constitute a 
 Cross Default under this Agreement, entitling the Lender to immediately accelerate 
 the loan and demand full repayment within 48 hours. 
 5.3. Absolute Assignment of Receivables and IP 
 As additional security, the Borrower hereby executes an ab initio absolute assignment 
 of all its present and future trade receivables. Furthermore, in the event of default, the 
 Borrower automatically assigns all rights, title, and interest in its proprietary patents 
 (Patent No. IN-2023-887766 for 'Thermal Forging Process') to the Lender without 
 requirement of further deed or court order. 
 5.4. Unilateral Right to Debit and Set-Off 
 The Lender shall have a paramount, continuous, and overarching right of set-off and 
 lien, independent of any other encumbrance, over all deposits, fixed deposits (FD 
 Receipt No. FDR-99887766), mutual fund units, and any other assets of the Borrower 
 and Guarantor held with any financial institution in India, to appropriate towards the 
 outstanding dues without prior notice. 
 P A G E 6 -- F O R M A T B R E A K I n i t i a l s : ______ I 
 n i t i a l s : ______ 
 6. SECURITY AND HYPOTHECATION 
 The Facility shall be secured by: 
 6.1. Primary Security: A first pari passu charge by way of hypothecation over all entire 
 current assets of the Borrower, including raw materials, work-in-progress, finished 
 goods, consumable stores, and spares lying at the Vatva plant (Property ID: 
 VAT-99-GIDC). 
 6.2. Collateral Security: A registered equitable mortgage over the commercial real 
 estate owned by the Guarantor located at Plot 44, SG Highway, Ahmedabad (Survey 
 No. 112/A, Khata No. 889). 
 6.3. Personal Guarantee: The absolute, irrevocable, and unconditional personal 
 guarantee of Mrs. Savitri Chattopadhyay. The Guarantor agrees that her liability is 
 co-extensive with the Borrower and the Lender may proceed directly against her 
 without first exhausting remedies against the Borrower. 
 7. FORCE MAJEURE EXCEPTIONS (Pro-Lender) 
 The obligations stipulated under Clause 3 (Interest & Payments) and Clause 4 
 (Obligations) shall continue mutatis mutandis notwithstanding any Force Majeure 
 event. The Borrower's obligation to repay borrowed money and accrue interest is 
 absolute and shall not be suspended by any pandemic, lockdown, government order, 
 or "Act of God". 
 8. EVENTS OF DEFAULT 
 The occurrence of any of the following shall constitute an Event of Default: 
 8.1. Failure to pay any principal, interest, overdue charge, hidden fee, or penalty on 
 the exact due date.

documents.page_content:
8.2. Any representation, warranty, or financial statement (including the Audit Report 
 dated 12.V.2025) is found to be incorrect, misleading, or deceptive prima facie . 
 8.3. The Borrower’s net worth erodes by more than 15% in any single financial 
 quarter. 
 8.4. A distress, execution, attachment, or other legal process is levied against any 
 asset of the Borrower (e.g., Seizure of Vehicle Number GJ-01-XX-9999 by RTO 
 authorities). 
 8.5. The Guarantor is declared insolvent, attempts to alienate the mortgaged property, 
 or passes away. 
 9. GOVERNING LAW, ARBITRATION AND JURISDICTION 
 9.1. Governing Law 
 This Agreement shall be governed by and construed strictly in accordance with the 
 laws of the Republic of India. 
 9.2. Arbitration (Mandatory and Asymmetric) 
 Notwithstanding anything contained in the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996, any 
 dispute, controversy, or claim arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, 
 including any question regarding its existence, validity, or termination, shall be 
 referred to and finally resolved by arbitration. 
 Trap: The arbitral tribunal shall consist of a Sole Arbitrator to be unilaterally 
 appointed by the Lender. The seat, venue, and place of arbitration shall exclusively be 
 GIFT City, Gandhinagar, Gujarat. All proceedings shall be in English. The Borrower 
 explicitly waives its right to seek interim relief from any civil court. 
 9.3. Exclusive Jurisdiction 
 Subject to the arbitration clause above, the courts at Ahmedabad alone shall have 
 exclusive jurisdiction to try and entertain any disputes. The Borrower expressly 
 waives the defense of forum non conveniens . 
 10. AMENDMENTS AND REVISIONS 
 10.1. Amendment No.1 (Pre-Execution Insertion) 
 It is mutually agreed that Clause 3.2(a) stands amended ab initio . The Processing Fee 
 is hereby increased from 2.5% to 3.0% (₹ 1,35,00,000) effective immediately. 
 10.2. Deleted Clause 
 Clause 4.1(f) regarding "Borrower's right to request interest rate reduction" is hereby 
 deleted in its entirety and superseded by Clause 3.1(c). 
 10.3. Inserted Clause 8.6 
 "If the Borrower attempts to refinance this Facility through any other institution, a 
 'Refinancing Sabotage Penalty' of 8% of the sanctioned limit shall become 
 immediately due and payable."

executive_summary:
The key obligations of each party under the Agreement are multifaceted. The Borrower is required to maintain specific financial ratios, including a Current Ratio of 1.33:1 and a Total Outside Liabilities to Tangible Net Worth ratio of 2.5:1. Additionally, the Borrower must ensure all cash flows are routed through the Lender's Escrow Account and submit audited financials and GST Returns. The Borrower is also prohibited from undertaking certain business activities without the Lender's consent.

The key financial terms and conditions of the Agreement dictate that the Borrower repay the loan with interest. The Lender has significant control over the Borrower's assets, including the right to debit and set-off, and may accelerate loan repayment in the event of default. The Borrower is subject to various penalties, including a refinancing sabotage penalty, non-refundable fees, and punitive charges for overdue payments. The Lender also has the right to unilaterally reset the interest rate.

The top identified risks and liabilities under the Agreement include the Borrower's potential failure to maintain the required financial ratios, default on loan repayment, and undertaking prohibited business activities. The Lender's rights are further protected through asymmetric arbitration and governing law clauses, which may pose a risk to the Borrower in the event of a dispute. The Guarantor and Security Trustee also have obligations under the Agreement, which may impact the Borrower's liabilities.
